summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #297/#298/#299 in the ars-ui Rust frontend library, creating framework-agnostic core implementations of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue type in ars-core, with adapter work explicitly out of scope.
summary.outcome: All 8 components were implemented with full test coverage and specs reconciled; PR #698 was created with CI green after 11 rounds of Codex review addressing 53 findings covering controlled-mode consistency, accessibility, RTL, edge cases, and API correctness.

repo: core_maths::CoreFloat is the workspace's no_std float tool requiring fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x); it lacks min/max so private fmin/fmax helpers are needed
CssProperty::Custom renders as --{name} so the name must NOT include the -- prefix
Service::send is &mut self; Service::new panics on empty Props.id
Messages must derive PartialEq for Context PartialEq to work
TransitionPlan closures need explicit type annotation |ctx: &mut Context| to avoid E0282
Effects are NOT run by send() — must call effect.run(svc.context(), svc.props(), send) separately
Bindable<T>::get() returns the controlled/parent value; pending() returns the internal/set value — critical distinction for drag machines
generate_id() doesn't exist in the codebase; use consumer-supplied props.id per meter/stat component convention
MessageFn only has From<F> impls for a fixed set of argument signatures — new signatures require adding From<F> impls to message_fn.rs
ComponentPart derive doesn't work with f64 fields (not Eq/Hash) — AngleSlider's Marker{value: f64} needs manual impl using to_bits()
The workspace denies clippy::suspicious — stable 1.96 surfaced a pre-existing map_or_else lint in pagination/mod.rs
spec-compile-snippets gate compiles spec code blocks in isolation; specs can diverge from codebase if no_check fenced
track_pointer_drag and use_platform_effects are referenced in specs but do NOT exist in the codebase — adapter-driven drag is the real pattern
Hue values wrap via ColorValue::new into [0,360) — the 360 degree endpoint is inaccessible unless slider_value/hue_value is tracked separately from the ColorValue
trim_start_matches('#') strips ALL leading # characters; strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) is the correct idiom
f64::clamp panics on min>max or NaN bounds — a safe wrapper is needed
Bindable::pending() had to be added as a new method (it didn't exist previously)

code.finding: From<F> impls for custom message signatures; new ones were added for color component message types
Bindable<T>: get() returns controlled prop, pending() returns internal value — critical distinction for controlled-mode display and ARIA
new() only debug_asserts finiteness; release builds allowed NaN — fixed with is_finite() coercion
from_hex sliced bytes without ASCII guard — non-ASCII input panicked
parse functions accepted NaN/inf via parse::<f64>().ok()
parse_hsb_args fed out-of-range S/B into math before clamping
HSB format omitted alpha channel, breaking round-trips for translucent colors
format_value showed percentages but commit_input parsed them as fractions (0-1), silently corrupting saturation on commit
IME Change arm ran before readonly/disabled guards
SetHasDescription was dropped by the disabled guard
SetProps updated channel/format but left input_text in old representation
spinbutton aria-valuenow/min/max exposed 0-1 range for percentage channels instead of 0-100
alpha gradient first stop was transparent (black) instead of the color at alpha=0
RTL position mirroring applied in render but pointer drag was not inverted
used % instead of rem_euclid, giving negative hue on large decrements
thumb had aria-labelledby referencing a non-existent Label part
SetProps left value outside new min/max bounds after updating constraints
f64::clamp panicked on min>max or NaN bounds
columns:0 made FocusUp/Down a no-op
idle state had no tabbable item — all items at tabindex=-1, making keyboard entry impossible
pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else introduced as deny in stable 1.96; required investigation and fix before CI could pass

workflow: cargo xci-fast is the pre-push gate covering 10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count
cargo xtask spec validate validates all spec frontmatter and cross-references
cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace is the exact CI coverage command; --workspace boosts ars-components branch coverage ~1.1% above -p alone
ars-components branch coverage threshold is 90%; one-armed if flag { ... } conditionals need BOTH arms tested
snapshot-count gate requires at least 1 snapshot per state variant
Spec must be reconciled in the same PR as the implementation per spec-first rule in CLAUDE.md
Spawning a specialized spec-sync agent (read-only, targeted) is efficient for mirroring code changes into markdown spec files
waiting-for-codex-review skill manages the @codex review to thumbs-up lifecycle via polling reactions and thread state
Codex usage limits can block reviews; quota must reset before retrying
Controlled mode pattern: set() updates internal state, get() returns controlled prop — any display/ARIA/form-submit reading get() will lag behind in-progress user interaction
zsh: the `status` variable is read-only; use a different name in background watcher scripts

friction: Codex rate limits blocked pass 1, requiring manual re-triggering after quota reset
11 rounds of Codex review (53 findings) on agnostic core with no adapter consumer yet, with no clear convergence signal
CoreFloat missing min/max required discovering and implementing private fmin/fmax helpers
Specs described non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects, track_pointer_drag) requiring full reconciliation
Branch coverage failure (89.7% < 90%) after initial implementation due to uncovered one-armed conditionals
Pre-existing pagination clippy lint on stable 1.96 was an unexpected blocker requiring investigation and approval before fixing
f64 Eq/Hash limitation caused a compiler error requiring a manual ComponentPart impl for AngleSlider
zsh read-only `status` variable crashed a background watcher script, requiring a rewrite
Type inference E0282 in TransitionPlan closures required annotating all |ctx| parameters
The controlled-mode display bug (get() vs pending()) was pervasive across 6 machines and required multiple passes to fully address
open_items: Codex thumbs-up not yet received as of session end — PR #698 still awaiting approval for merge
Issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge but are still open pending PR merge
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*) describe future adapter implementation — explicitly deferred
ColorValue spec could be promoted from color-picker.md Types section to spec/shared/color-types.md — deferred as orthogonal
block v0.1.6 future-incompat transitive-dep warning is pre-existing and unaddressed
Codex may surface additional findings in passes 12+ as round 11 was still in progress at session end
